newsSinceJun6: May 2026 monthly revenue of NT$4.20B, +3.13% MoM and +33.0% YoY (cumulative 2026 revenue NT$19.38B, +29.3% YoY) - solid but in line with the established ABF/HPC growth trajectory. No new contracts, design wins, M&A, or guidance changes since June 6. Background: Q2 substrate price hikes (~5%) and the NT$23.5B (~US$744M) ABF capacity expansion approved in February 2026 remain the standing fundamental drivers; brokers had already upgraded ratings on ABF demand visibility before this window.

sources.label: Win Invest - Kinsus (3189) monthly revenue (May 2026 NT$4.20B, +33% YoY)
DIGITIMES - Kinsus approves US$744M ABF expansion on three-year demand outlook (Feb 2026 background)
UDN Money - brokers raise Kinsus rating and earnings estimates on ABF demand visibility

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved Kinsus down for the +358% YTD rerating; the only new datapoint since June 6 is May revenue (+33% YoY), which confirms the trajectory but adds no hidden alpha, so no second consecutive downgrade is warranted. Small negative drift as the stock grinds another +2.3% on no thesis-changing news, further compressing residual asymmetry.
verdicts.bull: ABF substrate scarcity for AI GPUs/ASICs is real and tightening: Q2 price hikes of ~5%, Ibiden projecting high-end ABF demand area up 275% by 2027, and Kinsus's NT$23.5B expansion gives it volume leverage into 2027-2028.
verdicts.neutral: May revenue +33% YoY is healthy but in line; at $11.7B market cap and +358% YTD the market already prices substrate tightness, leaving Kinsus as a fairly-valued cyclical beneficiary rather than a hidden bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Kinsus is widely seen as the weakest of Taiwan's ABF trio (behind Unimicron and Nan Ya PCB); a substrate pricing cycle rollover or 2027 capacity waves from Ibiden/peers would hit a name that has already rerated 4.5x, with monthly revenue any miss now a sharp de-rating trigger.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 earnings and continued substrate price hikes into H2 2026; first evidence the Yangmei Plant 6 ABF lines are sold out ahead of 2027 ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded post-rerating positioning: any monthly revenue deceleration or ABF pricing pause triggers an outsized pullback given the +358% YTD run.
